_comment: Allowlist of acronyms / initialisms found in PM company names. The Dwellsy source CSV title-cases names at the source ('PMI Mile High' → 'Pmi Mile High'), losing the acronym capitalization. pm_name_normalize.py walks each PM name token-by-token and restores any token that case-insensitively matches an entry here to the canonical casing in this list. Entries are case-sensitive — 'PMI' here means the output should be 'PMI', not 'Pmi' or 'pmi'. Add new entries as they're spotted (the audit_pm_names.py script grep-scans the data for suspects). Two heuristic rules supplement this list: (1) any 2-character all-letter token gets uppercased automatically (high confidence — 'Wh', 'Pm', 'Jb' are essentially never English words), and (2) tokens listed in canonical_decisions_*.json's nameOverrides field win over both the allowlist and the heuristic.
_stopwords_comment: 2-letter English words that should NOT be uppercased by the auto-upper-2-char rule. The auto rule treats any 2-char all-letter token as an acronym; these are the exceptions that are real English words and would create false positives ('Mansion At Bala' → 'Mansion AT Bala' is wrong). State codes (TX, FL, WA, CO, etc.) that happen to spell English words (IN, OR, OK, ME, HI, NO) default to staying lowercase here — those collisions are rarer than the prepositions/conjunctions they conflict with. If a specific state-code-as-token case shows up wrong, use the per-PM nameOverrides mechanism.
